segments.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord, high and exalted, seated on a throne; and the train of his robe filled the temple.
Above him were seraphim, each with six wings: With two wings they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they were flying.
And they were calling to one another:
Holy, holy, holy is the Lord Almighty; the whole earth is full of his glory.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: At the sound of their voices the doorposts and thresholds shook and the temple was filled with smoke.
Woe to me! I am ruined! For I am a man of unclean lips, and I live among a people of unclean lips, and my eyes have seen the King, the Lord Almighty.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Then one of the seraphim flew to me with a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with tongs from the altar.
With it he touched my mouth and said,
See, this has touched your lips; your guilt is taken away and your sin atoned for.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying,
Whom shall I send? And who will go for us?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Here am I. Send me!
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: He said,
Go and tell this people: 'Be ever hearing, but never understanding; be ever seeing, but never perceiving.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Make the heart of this people calloused; make their ears dull and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn and be healed.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then I said,
For how long, Lord?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: And he answered:
Until the cities lie ruined and without inhabitant, until the houses are left deserted and the fields ruined and ravaged,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Until the Lord has sent everyone far away and the land is utterly forsaken.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And though a tenth remains in the land, it will again be laid waste. But as the terebinth and oak leave stumps when they are cut down, so the holy seed will be the stump in the land.
